what_it_does: This file is a research synthesis map that consolidates eight prior reports into a single V-surface map, cataloguing code/design substrates, their type/registry/resolver status, reuse versus build decisions, explicit conflicts, cross-dimensional seams, and future possibilities for a resolution-first interface surface.
observations: Lists eight source reports/dimensions: v-slot-socket, mode-colour, archetypes-atomic, sequences-stories-slides, gallery-app, channels-integration, resolution-spine, operator-surface-RHM.
Uses evidence tiers: BUILT, observed-in-code, DESIGNED-not-built, claimed-not-verifiable.
Contains a table mapping named things (mode, V/slot/socket, window, expansion-setting, sub-surface, decision, option, decision-card) to type/scheme, resolver/registry, schema, and status.
Identifies three explicit conflicts: gold-drift in mode-colour, a claimed-not-verifiable built assertion, and two un-unified channel stacks.
Details a mode-colour cascade with six build steps and a single proposed CSS token --vi-mode-primary.
Describes recursive nesting of V sockets as already latent via mountConnection/renderDefinition.
Provides a 32-item seams table (S1-S32) relating pairs of dimensions to exact file:line joins.
Includes a grouped BEYOND list (B1-B7) covering self-build closure, future surfaces, memory, sequences, DNA completion, type-system deepening, and V polish.
Ends with an end-to-end relational spine diagram from Principal aim through resolve_address to rendered V surface.
Contains no code symbols; symbols[] is empty per the prompt.
standouts: Resolution-first spine: every named thing is a type that resolves through one resolver (resolve_address).
Mode-colour gold-drift conflict: three different hexes claimed for the same canonical gold role.
Primary architectural gap: two channel stacks (cc_channels vs session_channels) with no unification layer.
Recursive V nesting is structurally available today with zero mechanism changes.
32 seams table treats relationships as the primary content of the map.
conventions: Preserves verbatim file:line citations from source reports.
Tags every claim with an evidence tier (BUILT, observed-in-code, DESIGNED-not-built, claimed-not-verifiable).
Follows an additive schema law: new fields optional-with-defaults, breaking changes as versioned side-by-side shapes.
Prefers registry rows over hand-placed markup or restyle.
Uses address-as-identity across composition, backend, and memory layers.
Maintains neutral synthesis without ranking dimensions.
concerns: Several items labeled DESIGNED-not-built could be mistaken for implemented if readers skip status labels.
A claim about --vi-mode-primary being built is explicitly marked claimed-not-verifiable because the composition runtime is not accessible in the local filesystem.
Gold-drift conflict is surfaced but not resolved in the file.
The two channel stacks are left un-unified; the file warns not to smooth the gap prematurely.
High information density may make the map hard to maintain without tooling.
notes: A dense, citation-rich integration map of the V surface's eight dimensions, intended to guide build decisions by showing what exists, what must be built, and how the pieces seam together.
questions: Which DESIGNED-not-built items already have partial runtime code that could be promoted?
How will the gold-drift conflict among #E3C421, #D4AF37, and #e6ab5c be resolved?
What is the exact intended boundary between cc_channels and session_channels if they remain separate?
Are the eight source reports themselves available for direct verification?
How does the proposed window:// scheme relate to the existing live/empty mark-type rows?
What process will enforce the one required component.schema.json change for connection?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to synthesize the eight reports into a V-surface research map; actual content matches by cataloguing dimensions, statuses, conflicts, reuse/build boundaries, seams, and future work.
apparent_themes: Common memory across sessions and channels
intention_signals: To converge a sprawling design space into an actionable build map; to demonstrate that the V surface can grow by adding registry rows rather than screen code; to surface conflicts and gaps explicitly instead of hiding them; to articulate a single relational spine end-to-end.
novelty: The synthesis of eight substrate reports into one map with a seams-as-content table and explicit evidence tiers; treating mode-colour as a resolved token cascade rather than a restyle; framing recursive V nesting as already latent.
contribution: A structured, citation-preserving integration document that records status labels, conflicts, reuse versus build boundaries, and cross-dimensional seams, which a fused system would keep as a build-prep reference and audit trail.
summary_for_embedding: A research synthesis map of the V surface's eight code/design dimensions, cataloguing type/registry status, reuse versus build work, explicit conflicts, seams, and future possibilities with verbatim file:line citations.
intention_for_embedding: To consolidate prior reports into a single actionable, evidence-tiered build map for the resolution-first V surface.
